description: Fish species for Sitka Sound fishing ecosystem - 50+ species across all biomes

species.name: Pink Salmon
species.description: The smallest and most abundant Pacific salmon, known locally as 'humpies' for their distinctive spawning hump.
species.lore: Every odd year, millions of pink salmon return to Sitka's streams, turning the waters silver with their spawning run.

species.name: Chum Salmon
species.description: Also called 'dog salmon' for their canine-like teeth during spawning season.
species.lore: Prized for its roe, chum salmon roe is a delicacy in Japanese cuisine as 'ikura'.

species.name: Pacific Herring
species.description: Small, silvery fish that form massive schools in Sitka Sound.
species.lore: Herring eggs on hemlock branches are a traditional Tlingit delicacy called 'xaak'.

species.name: Pacific Cod
species.description: A bottom-dwelling fish with a distinctive barbel on its chin.
species.lore: Pacific cod has been a commercial staple of Southeast Alaska for over a century.

species.name: Walleye Pollock
species.description: The world's largest food fishery, used for fish sticks, surimi, and fillets.
species.lore: Despite its humble reputation, pollock supports an entire industry and countless families.

species.name: Sand Dab
species.description: A small flatfish that hides in sandy ocean bottoms.
species.lore: Sand dabs are so well camouflaged that predators often swim right over them.

species.name: Redtail Surfperch
species.description: A colorful fish found in shallow coastal waters and surf zones.
species.lore: Surfperch give birth to live young, a rarity among fish.

species.name: Starry Flounder
species.description: A flatfish with distinctive star-like patterns, tolerant of both salt and freshwater.
species.lore: Unlike most flounder, starry flounder can be either right-eyed or left-eyed.

species.name: Brook Trout
species.description: A beautiful freshwater trout with distinctive red spots and white-edged fins.
species.lore: Brook trout are actually char, not true trout, and thrive in cold, clean waters.

species.name: Arctic Grayling
species.description: Recognized by its enormous, sail-like dorsal fin and iridescent colors.
species.lore: Grayling can live up to 30 years in Alaska's cold, pristine waters.

species.name: Lake Trout
species.description: A deep-water char species, the largest trout native to Alaska.
species.lore: Lake trout can live over 50 years and some specimens have weighed over 100 pounds.

species.name: Northern Pike
species.description: A fearsome predator with a duckbill snout and rows of sharp teeth.
species.lore: Pike have been known to attack small mammals and even waterfowl.

species.name: Burbot
species.description: The only freshwater cod, also called 'ling' or 'eelpout' for its snakelike body.
species.lore: Burbot liver is rich in vitamins and considered a delicacy in some cultures.

species.name: Sculpin
species.description: A small, bottom-dwelling fish with a large head and spiny fins.
species.lore: Sculpin have no swim bladder and sink immediately when they stop swimming.

species.name: Alaska Blackfish
species.description: A hardy fish that can survive in oxygen-poor water and even breathe air.
species.lore: Alaska Natives traditionally kept blackfish alive in buckets for weeks during winter.

species.name: Broad Whitefish
species.description: A silvery fish prized for its delicate, white flesh.
species.lore: Whitefish are a crucial food source for both humans and bears in Alaska.

species.name: Arctic Char
species.description: A cold-water fish with brilliant red-orange spawning colors.
species.lore: Arctic char can survive in waters so cold that other fish would freeze solid.

species.name: Cave Sculpin
species.description: A pale, eyeless fish adapted to complete darkness.
species.lore: These fish navigate using their lateral line to detect vibrations in the water.

species.name: Blind Cave Loach
species.description: A tiny, translucent fish that has evolved without eyes.
species.lore: Their skin is so thin you can see their internal organs working.

species.name: Coho Salmon
species.description: Also known as silver salmon, prized for their fighting spirit and bright silver sides.
species.lore: Coho are the acrobats of the salmon world, often leaping clear of the water when hooked.

species.name: Rainbow Trout
species.description: A beautiful freshwater fish with a distinctive pink stripe along its side.
species.lore: Steelhead, the ocean-going form of rainbow trout, return to spawn multiple times in their lives.

species.name: Dolly Varden
species.description: A char species named after a Charles Dickens character, known for its spotted beauty.
species.lore: During salmon spawning season, Dolly Varden feast on loose eggs, growing fat and flavorful.

species.name: Lingcod
species.description: A voracious predator with a massive head and sharp teeth, prized for its firm white meat.
species.lore: Female lingcod fiercely guard their egg nests, and divers report them being aggressive when approached.

species.name: Pacific Flounder
species.description: A flatfish that lies camouflaged on the ocean floor, both eyes on one side of its head.
species.lore: Flounder begin life swimming upright, but one eye migrates to the other side as they mature.

species.name: Quillback Rockfish
species.description: A deep-water rockfish with venomous spines and distinctive quill-like fins.
species.lore: Quillbacks can live over 90 years, slowly growing in the cold depths.

species.name: Black Rockfish
species.description: A common nearshore rockfish with dark coloration and mild flesh.
species.lore: Black rockfish give birth to live young and can produce up to a million larvae per spawn.

species.name: Alaskan Sole
species.description: A small flatfish found in deeper waters, prized for its delicate flavor.
species.lore: Sole are so flat they seem to be only two dimensions when viewed from the side.

species.name: Coastal Cutthroat Trout
species.description: Named for the distinctive red-orange slash under their jaw.
species.lore: Cutthroat trout are extremely territorial and will aggressively defend their feeding lies.

species.name: Steelhead
species.description: The ocean-going rainbow trout, legendary among anglers for its fighting ability.
species.lore: Steelhead can return to spawn multiple times, unlike Pacific salmon which die after spawning.

species.name: Channel Catfish
species.description: A whiskered bottom-feeder with excellent taste and smell senses.
species.lore: Catfish have over 100,000 taste buds all over their bodies, making them living tongues.

species.name: Largemouth Bass
species.description: A popular game fish with an enormous mouth that can swallow prey whole.
species.lore: Largemouth bass have been introduced worldwide, becoming invasive in some ecosystems.

species.name: Longnose Gar
species.description: A prehistoric-looking fish with a long, tooth-filled snout and armored scales.
species.lore: Gar have swim bladders that function like lungs, allowing them to gulp air at the surface.

species.name: Bowfin
species.description: A living fossil, unchanged for over 100 million years.
species.lore: Male bowfins guard their nests fiercely and will attack anything that approaches.

species.name: Shadow Bass
species.description: A pale bass species that has adapted to underground waters.
species.lore: Their sensitive lateral lines can detect a single insect falling on the water's surface.

species.name: Luminous Minnow
species.description: A tiny fish that produces its own light through bioluminescence.
species.lore: Schools of glow minnows light up underground lakes like underwater stars.

species.name: Ice Perch
species.description: A hardy fish found in icy waters, active even under ice cover.
species.lore: Ice perch produce natural antifreeze proteins that prevent their blood from freezing.

species.name: Sockeye Salmon
species.description: The red salmon, known for its deep red flesh and brilliant spawning colors.
species.lore: Sockeye navigate back to their birth streams using the Earth's magnetic field and their sense of smell.

species.name: Pacific Halibut
species.description: A massive flatfish that can grow over 400 pounds, the ultimate bottom fish prize.
species.lore: Known as 'barn doors' when they reach trophy size, halibut can live over 50 years.

species.name: Yelloweye Rockfish
species.description: A deep-water fish with striking orange-red coloration, locally called 'red snapper'.
species.lore: Yelloweye rockfish can live over 100 years, making them one of the longest-lived fishes.

species.name: King Salmon
species.description: The Chinook salmon, largest of all Pacific salmon, the king of game fish.
species.lore: A 97-pound king salmon caught in the Kenai River still holds the world record.

species.name: Dungeness Crab
species.description: A prized shellfish with sweet, tender meat, named after Dungeness, Washington.
species.lore: Dungeness crabs molt up to 15 times before reaching harvest size, a process called 'soft-shell'.

species.name: White Sturgeon
species.description: A prehistoric giant, the largest freshwater fish in North America.
species.lore: Sturgeon have remained virtually unchanged for 200 million years, true living dinosaurs.

species.name: Tiger Muskellunge
species.description: A rare hybrid of muskie and northern pike, with striking barred patterns.
species.lore: Tiger muskie are sterile, putting all their energy into aggressive growth.

species.name: Golden Trout
species.description: One of the most colorful freshwater fish, with brilliant gold, red, and olive bands.
species.lore: Golden trout are only found in high mountain lakes, making them a prize for dedicated anglers.

species.name: Ancient Catfish
species.description: A massive, blind catfish that has lived in underground waters for millennia.
species.lore: These catfish can go years between feedings, surviving on the slow trickle of nutrients from above.

species.name: Frost Pike
species.description: A white-scaled pike variant found only in the coldest waters.
species.lore: Frost pike are said to appear only when the aurora borealis dances overhead.

species.name: Sablefish
species.description: Also known as black cod or butterfish, with incredibly rich, buttery flesh.
species.lore: Sablefish contain more omega-3 fatty acids than almost any other fish, making them both delicious and nutritious.

species.name: Alaskan Spot Prawn
species.description: The largest shrimp in the Pacific Northwest, sweet and tender with a delicate texture.
species.lore: Spot prawns are hermaphroditic, starting life as males and transitioning to females in their third year.

species.name: Copper River King
species.description: The most prized salmon in the world, swimming 300 miles up the treacherous Copper River.
species.lore: Their high oil content comes from storing energy for the longest salmon migration on Earth.

species.name: Opah Moonfish
species.description: A rare, circular fish with brilliant silver and red coloration, the only truly warm-blooded fish.
species.lore: Opah generate heat by constantly flapping their wing-like pectoral fins, maintaining body temperature above the surrounding water.

species.name: Pacific Bluefin Tuna
species.description: The ultimate game fish, capable of incredible speed and legendary fights.
species.lore: Bluefin tuna can swim at 50 mph and cross entire oceans in search of food.

species.name: Alligator Gar
species.description: A massive, armored fish with two rows of teeth and a reptilian appearance.
species.lore: Alligator gar have existed for 100 million years and can breathe air through their swim bladder.

species.name: Cave Stalker
species.description: An apex predator of underground waters, blind but with heightened senses.
species.lore: Cave stalkers can detect a single drop of water falling from the ceiling from across an underground lake.

species.name: Aurora Char
species.description: A char that only appears when the northern lights are visible, glowing with their colors.
species.lore: Tlingit legend says aurora char are the spirits of ancestors swimming in the sky's reflection.

species.name: Spirit of the Sound
species.description: An albino king salmon said to be the spirit of Tlingit ancestors returning to watch over the waters.
species.lore: Legend says seeing a ghost salmon brings either great fortune or great change to the observer.

species.name: Leviathan of the Deep
species.description: A halibut of mythical proportions, older than most trees and wiser than most fishermen.
species.lore: The Tlingit tell of Halibut Man, a being who could transform between human and fish form to commune with the ocean depths.

species.name: Aurora's Blessing
species.description: A rainbow trout that glows with the colors of the aurora borealis, only appearing under northern lights.
species.lore: Caught only when the sky dances with green fire, Aurora's Blessing is said to grant the angler visions of future catches.

specialEffect.description: Reveal fish locations on your map for 48 hours

species.name: Elder Sturgeon
species.description: A sturgeon over a century old, covered in ancient scars and bearing the wisdom of generations.
species.lore: Some elder sturgeon were alive when the Tlingit first settled these lands 10,000 years ago.

specialEffect.description: Double XP from all fishing activities for 3 days

species.name: Void Swimmer
species.description: A fish that exists between dimensions, phasing in and out of reality.
species.lore: Void swimmers are said to have been swimming in underground waters since before the world was formed.

specialEffect.description: 50% chance to avoid losing fish on failed catch for 24 hours

species.name: Frost Serpent
species.description: A serpentine fish of pure ice, appearing only during the coldest winter nights.
species.lore: Legend says the Frost Serpent was born from the first winter and carries the cold of eternity in its scales.

specialEffect.description: Fish bite slower (easier catches) for 48 hours
